name: KOS Generator
description: Autonomous generation rules for producing valid kos.json files. Any AI system that reads this document can generate structured, provenance-tracked knowledge for any entity in any industry.
license: Copyright (c) 2026 Niseus Authority Systems. Open specification. Free to implement. Attribution required.

usage.for_ai_agents: Fetch this file. Follow the steps in order. Output a valid kos.json. No additional software or human input required.
usage.for_automated_pipelines: Parse this JSON programmatically. Extract steps, rules, and mappings. Feed to any AI system as structured instructions.
usage.for_humans: Read the steps below. They describe the complete generation algorithm in both structured and natural language.

steps.name: identify_entity
steps.instruction: Determine the entity's canonical identity from the input provided (URL, name, or description). Research if needed.

output.name: Display name of the entity
output.type: One of: Organization, LocalBusiness, Person, Product, Event, CreativeWork
output.id: Format: kos:{slug} — lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens

steps.name: discover_knowledge
steps.instruction: Gather all available public information about the entity. Use every accessible source. Record which sources provided which information — this determines confidence scoring.

sources.name: entity_website
sources.description: The entity's own website (home, about, services, contact, FAQ pages)

sources.name: structured_data
sources.description: Schema.org JSON-LD or microdata already on the entity's website

sources.name: business_directory
sources.description: Google Business Profile, Apple Maps, Bing Places, or equivalent

sources.name: public_records
sources.description: Government databases: licenses, incorporations, registrations, permits

steps.name: map_to_nodes
steps.instruction: For each distinct piece of knowledge discovered, create a typed node. Use the core taxonomy for standard types. Use namespaced types (e.g., food:menu-item, legal:practice-area) when the core types are insufficient.

node_mapping.if_found: A physical address, region, or coverage area
A license, certification, award, or accreditation
A team member, owner, or key person
A product for sale or distribution
An FAQ, article, guide, or published material

node_requirements.id: kos:{descriptive-slug} — unique within document, lowercase, hyphens only
node_requirements.type: From core taxonomy or namespaced extension
node_requirements.label: Clear, human-readable name
node_requirements.description: 1-3 sentences. Write for both humans and machines.
node_requirements.properties: Optional. Type-specific structured data (hours, prices, areas, license numbers).

steps.name: assign_provenance
steps.instruction: This is the most critical step. Every node MUST have honest provenance. Never inflate confidence. The integrity of the entire protocol depends on honest self-assessment.

source.reason: Always 'automated' for AI-generated content. This is non-negotiable.

method_vocabulary.when: Extracted from existing Schema.org or JSON-LD
Obtained from a structured API or database
Deduced from context, not explicitly stated

confidence_table.condition: Found in one structured source (Schema.org, GBP)
Found in one structured source with detail
Found in two sources, at least one structured
Found in three or more sources, or confirmed by public record

hard_cap.reason: The range 0.8-1.0 is reserved exclusively for human-verified data. An AI-generated kos.json MUST NEVER assign confidence above 0.8. This boundary is inviolable.

steps.name: create_relationships
steps.instruction: Identify connections between nodes. Only create relationships that are evident from the source data. Do not infer speculative connections.

relationship_types.semantics: Source depends on target. Target must be true/valid for source to hold.
relationship_types.example: A service depends on a license being active

relationship_types.semantics: Source and target are semantically related. Bidirectional.

common_patterns.pattern: Service requires a license or credential
FAQ answers a question about a service
Service is available in a location
Product is sold at a location

steps.name: assemble_document
steps.instruction: Construct the complete kos.json document. Compute metadata. Set draft flags.

structure.knowledge: Array of all nodes from step 3
structure.relations: Array of all top-level relations from step 5 (inline relations go inside nodes)

meta.generator: Your AI model identifier (e.g., Claude/opus-4-6, GPT-4o, Gemini-2)
meta.avg_freshness: Computed mean of all node freshness values
meta.stale_count: Count of nodes with freshness below 0.5

steps.name: self_validate
steps.instruction: Before outputting, verify the document against these checks. Fix any failures. Do not output an invalid document.

checks.rule: Root keys $schema, entity, knowledge, meta all present
Every node has id, type, label, provenance
Every provenance has created and source
All relation targets reference existing node IDs or valid external URIs
At least one knowledge node exists
Node descriptions are present and under 1000 characters
meta.total_nodes matches actual knowledge array length

output_format.instruction: Output ONLY the valid JSON document. No commentary, no markdown fences, no explanation. Raw JSON ready to save as kos.json.

design_principles.universality: These rules work for any entity in any industry. The AI's contextual understanding handles domain adaptation. The protocol handles structure.
design_principles.honesty: A kos.json with 5 honest nodes is more valuable than 50 nodes with inflated confidence. Never fabricate. Never inflate. Gaps are acceptable; lies are not.
design_principles.autonomy: This generator requires no human intervention. An AI system fetches this file, reads the rules, and produces valid output. The protocol is self-executing.
design_principles.improvement: As AI systems improve, generation quality improves — without any change to this specification. The rules are stable. The runtime evolves.

meta.author: Niseus Authority Systems
